title: PNG backend text anchoring inconsistent with SVG
description: SkiaBackend::draw_text and draw_rotated_text interpret position.y as the top of the line box (cosmic_text default), while SvgBackend::draw_text emits SVG <text> where y is the baseline. Result: regenerated PNG snapshots after the layout overhaul show titles overlapping the plot area on top and X-axis titles clipped at the bottom canvas edge. SVG snapshots are visually correct. Fix: change SkiaBackend draw_text to subtract the line baseline offset (cosmic_text Buffer::layout_runs first run line_y, fallback font_size * 0.85) so position.y is the baseline. draw_rotated_text needs the same shift composed with the rotation transform: pre-translate buffer by (0, -ascent) so the baseline is the rotation pivot. After this lands, regenerate PNG snapshots only — SVG output is unchanged.
close_reason: Skia draw_text and draw_rotated_text now anchor at baseline matching SVG semantics, via line_y from cosmic_text Buffer::layout_runs (font_size*0.85 fallback)

title: Graph Margins
description: The current margin method is completely invalid. We need a way to account for needed space outside the graph render area so we can define the graph render area allowing for titles, axis labels, legends etc as well as long text or number ticks and things like color map range definitions. It should be highly modular and essentially invisible to the user, defined by what the user wants in the graph and automated from that. Want title, more top margin and a good placement etc. Tick string too long? more margin to avoid overlap etc
close_reason: Modular LayoutBuilder replaces hardcoded margins; legend/colormap reservations supported

title: chore: visual audit + polish pass over all gallery outputs
description: After all examples ship, run xtask gallery and audit every PNG (14 examples + 19 snapshots). Focus on text/labels/titles/margins. File one bd issue per cluster of related findings, then fix in this branch and regenerate snapshots in step 6.

title: feat: implement 11 ready-to-ship examples
description: Implement bar_chart, line_chart, scatter, heatmap, histogram, custom_theme, custom_colormap, quickstart, statistical, recipe, gallery as small (10-40 line) showcase examples in examples/showcases/. Each saves a PNG at examples/showcases/<name>.png. Honors the docstrings on the existing 5 non-empty stubs as intent hints.

title: feat: cargo xtask gallery subcommand
description: Implement xtask gallery to run all 17 examples and aggregate outputs at target/gallery/{examples,snapshots}/. Discovers examples by parsing [[example]] entries in examples/Cargo.toml. Snapshot PNGs are mirrored from starsight-layer-5/tests/snapshots/.

title: Edge gutter for tick label overhang
description: After replacing the hardcoded right_margin=20 with the LayoutBuilder, no built-in component reserves on the right side or on the top (when no title). The rightmost X tick label is centered on plot.right and the topmost Y tick label is centered on plot.top, so each can overhang the canvas edge by up to half its label width/height. Visible in snapshot_line_basic, snapshot_with_title_and_labels, and others — rightmost label clipped against canvas right edge. Fix: have XTickLabelsComponent return a second Reservation on the Right side sized as max_label_width / 2 + small buffer, and YTickLabelsComponent return a second Reservation on Top sized as max_label_height / 2 + small buffer. Composes correctly with future colorbar component on the right.
close_reason: XTickLabels reserves on right, YTickLabels reserves on top, sized as max_extent/2 + 2px; title bumped to priority 1 so Y top gutter sits flush against plot edge

title: Axis Labels
description: The current label system places the labels at the end of the axis and in the same space as ticks instead of center and below/to the side of the ticks.
close_reason: Tick labels centered using measured width, axis-title labels in dedicated slots

title: feat: placeholder PNGs for 6 backend-blocked examples
description: terminal, interactive, surface3d, streaming, polars_integration, faceting all need 0.3.0+ infrastructure that doesn't exist yet. Each saves a placeholder PNG using ordinary 2D Figure with a centered title naming the missing capability (e.g. 'interactive — demo lands in 0.3.0 (winit + wgpu backend)'). Registered in examples/Cargo.toml so xtask gallery runs every name uniformly.

title: Layout font size duplication between reserve and render
description: TitleComponent::reserve calls text_extent with title_font_size from LayoutCtx (currently 16). render_title hardcodes its own let font_size: f32 = 16.0. Same pattern for axis-title labels and tick labels. If anyone changes title_font_size in figures.rs but forgets to change the matching constant in renders.rs, the slot will be sized for one font and the text drawn at another. Fix: pass font sizes through Layout (e.g. on the Slot or as a separate field) so render helpers read from the same source as the layout calc.

title: Heatmap grid visibility
description: In all heatmap snapshots there are gray-ish lines in between each cell of color data, maybe due to overlap? Likely in heatmap mark rendering.
close_reason: Snapped heatmap cells to integer pixel boundaries; gray seams gone

title: Graph data margins
description: In example snapshot Population by city horizontal bar mark and some others the data extent and graph extent wildly don't match. Unsure why.
close_reason: BarMark::data_extent orientation-aware + Axis::category — bars fill plot area

title: Axis::category zero-input edge case
description: Axis::category(&[]) currently produces tick_positions=[0.0] and tick_labels=[""] with scale [0,0]. LinearScale::map with domain_max=domain_min returns 0.5 (intentional fallback), so all bars would map to the plot midpoint. Not reachable today (figures.rs checks !category_labels.is_empty() first), but worth a debug_assert!(!labels.is_empty()) in Axis::category to surface accidental misuse.

title: Document Axis::category category-axis bypass behavior
description: Bar marks bypass the scale on the category axis and use band-center math directly (area.left + (i + 0.5) * band_width). Axis::category has tick_positions at band edges, used only for grid lines (which intentionally fall between bars). The N+1 empty tick_labels are aligned with N+1 tick_positions to honor the 'one label per position' contract. Fix: add a doc comment on Axis::category making both invariants explicit, so a future contributor doesn't try to iterate tick_labels for categories.

title: Area Mark baseline
description: Currently the area mark absline snapshot in layer 5 shows the baseline as 30 but something makes 30 be the minimum height, most likely from the snapshot data. In essence we want the baseline to be not on the same line as the x axis to show below and above baseline rendering.
close_reason: Snapshot data crosses baseline; above/below rendering verified
